15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Bash Validation Utility Library
19	# Compatible with Bash 3.2 (e.g., macOS default shell)
20	
21	# Multiple inclusion guard
22	test -z "${ZBUV_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
23	ZBUV_INCLUDED=1
24	
25	# Literal constants — check capture output protocol markers
26	BUV_check_gated="gated"
27	BUV_check_fail="fail:"
28	
29	# Source the console utility library
30	ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
31	source "${ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
32	
33	buv_file_exists() {
34	  local z_filepath="${1:-}"
35	  test -f "${z_filepath}" || buc_die "Required file not found: ${z_filepath}"
36	}
37	
38	buv_dir_exists() {
39	  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
40	  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
41	}
42	
43	buv_dir_empty() {
44	  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
45	  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
46	  local z_check_file
47	  z_check_file=$(mktemp)
48	  find "${z_dirpath}" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -print -quit > "${z_check_file}"
49	  test ! -s "${z_check_file}" || { rm -f "${z_check_file}"; buc_die "Directory must be empty: ${z_dirpath}"; }
50	  rm -f "${z_check_file}"
51	}
52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Enrollment infrastructure
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	zbuv_kindle() {
58	  test -z "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buv already kindled"
59	
60	  # Enrollment rolls (9 parallel arrays)
61	  z_buv_scope_roll=()
62	  z_buv_varname_roll=()
63	  z_buv_type_roll=()
64	  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
65	  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
66	  z_buv_p1_roll=()
67	  z_buv_p2_roll=()
68	  z_buv_group_roll=()
69	  z_buv_desc_roll=()
70	
71	  # Group registry rolls (4 parallel arrays, foreign-keyed by group title)
72	  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
73	  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
74	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
75	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()
76	
77	  # Mutable kindle state: regime context (set by buv_regime_enroll / buv_group_enroll / buv_gate_enroll)
78	  z_buv_current_scope=""
79	  z_buv_current_group=""
80	  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
81	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
82	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
83	
84	  readonly ZBUV_KINDLED=1
85	}
86	
87	zbuv_sentinel() {
88	  test "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buv not kindled - call zbuv_kindle first"
89	}
90	
91	# Test support: reset enrollment state without re-kindling
92	zbuv_reset_enrollment() {
93	  zbuv_sentinel
94	
95	  # Clear enrollment rolls
96	  z_buv_scope_roll=()
97	  z_buv_varname_roll=()
98	  z_buv_type_roll=()
99	  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
100	  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
101	  z_buv_p1_roll=()
102	  z_buv_p2_roll=()
103	  z_buv_group_roll=()
104	  z_buv_desc_roll=()
105	
106	  # Clear group registry rolls
107	  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
108	  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
109	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
110	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()
111	
112	  # Reset regime context state
113	  z_buv_current_scope=""
114	  z_buv_current_group=""
115	  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
116	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
117	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
118	}
119	
120	# Regime context setters — called during kindle to establish enrollment scope
121	
122	# buv_regime_enroll SCOPE — set current enrollment scope
123	# Validates that SCOPE is non-empty. All subsequent enroll calls use this scope
124	# until another buv_regime_enroll is called.
125	buv_regime_enroll() {
126	  zbuv_sentinel
127	
128	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
129	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_regime_enroll: scope required"
130	  z_buv_current_scope="${z_scope}"
131	  z_buv_current_group=""
132	  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
133	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
134	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
135	}
136	
137	# buv_group_enroll TITLE — set current group context
138	# Creates a group registry entry. All subsequent enroll calls are tagged with
139	# this group title until the next buv_group_enroll call.
140	# Resets gate context — use buv_gate_enroll after this to gate items.
141	buv_group_enroll() {
142	  zbuv_sentinel
143	
144	  test -n "${z_buv_current_scope}" || buc_die "buv_group_enroll: call buv_regime_enroll first"
145	
146	  local z_title="${1:-}"
147	  test -n "${z_title}" || buc_die "buv_group_enroll: title required"
148	
149	  z_buv_grp_scope_roll+=("${z_buv_current_scope}")
150	  z_buv_grp_title_roll+=("${z_title}")
151	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll+=("")
152	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll+=("")
153	
154	  z_buv_current_group="${z_title}"
155	  z_buv_current_group_idx=$(( ${#z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]} - 1 ))
156	  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
157	  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
158	}
159	
160	# buv_gate_enroll GATE_VAR GATE_VAL — set gate context within current group
161	# All subsequent variable enrolls in this group are gated by this condition.
162	# Also registers the gate on the current group for render section headers.
163	buv_gate_enroll() {
164	  zbuv_sentinel
165	
166	  test -n "${z_buv_current_group}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: call buv_group_enroll first"
167	
168	  local z_gate_var="${1:-}"
169	  local z_gate_val="${2:-}"
170	  test -n "${z_gate_var}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: gate variable required"
171	  test -n "${z_gate_val}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: gate value required"
172	
173	  z_buv_current_gate_var="${z_gate_var}"
174	  z_buv_current_gate_val="${z_gate_val}"
175	
176	  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[z_buv_current_group_idx]="${z_gate_var}"
177	  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[z_buv_current_group_idx]="${z_gate_val}"
178	}
179	
180	# Internal enrollment helper — all public enroll functions delegate here
181	# Usage: zbuv_enroll VARNAME TYPE P1 P2 DESC
182	# Gate context inherited from buv_gate_enroll; group from buv_group_enroll.
183	zbuv_enroll() {
184	  zbuv_sentinel
185	
186	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
187	  local z_type="${2:-}"
188	  local z_p1="${3:-}"
189	  local z_p2="${4:-}"
190	  local z_desc="${5:-}"
191	
192	  test -n "${z_buv_current_scope}" || buc_die "zbuv_enroll: call buv_regime_enroll first"
193	  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] || buc_die "zbuv_enroll: invalid variable name: '${z_varname}'"
194	
195	  z_buv_scope_roll+=("${z_buv_current_scope}")
196	  z_buv_varname_roll+=("${z_varname}")
197	  z_buv_type_roll+=("${z_type}")
198	  z_buv_gate_var_roll+=("${z_buv_current_gate_var}")
199	  z_buv_gate_val_roll+=("${z_buv_current_gate_val}")
200	  z_buv_p1_roll+=("${z_p1}")
201	  z_buv_p2_roll+=("${z_p2}")
202	  z_buv_group_roll+=("${z_buv_current_group}")
203	  z_buv_desc_roll+=("${z_desc}")
204	}
205	
206	# Public enrollment functions — scalar types
207	#
208	# Signature: buv_TYPE_enroll VARNAME P1 P2 "description"
209	# Scope from buv_regime_enroll; group from buv_group_enroll; gate from buv_gate_enroll.
210	
211	buv_string_enroll() {
212	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
213	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
214	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
215	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
216	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "string" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
217	}
218	
219	buv_secret_enroll() {
220	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
221	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
222	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
223	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
224	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "secret" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
225	}
226	
227	buv_xname_enroll() {
228	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
229	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
230	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
231	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
232	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "xname" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
233	}
234	
235	buv_gname_enroll() {
236	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
237	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
238	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
239	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
240	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "gname" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
241	}
242	
243	buv_fqin_enroll() {
244	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
245	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
246	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
247	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
248	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "fqin" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
249	}
250	
251	buv_bool_enroll() {
252	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
253	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
254	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "bool" "" "" "${z_desc}"
255	}
256	
257	buv_enum_enroll() {
258	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
259	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
260	  shift 2
261	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "enum" "$*" "" "${z_desc}"
262	}
263	
264	buv_decimal_enroll() {
265	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
266	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
267	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
268	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
269	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "decimal" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
270	}
271	
272	buv_odref_enroll() {
273	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
274	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
275	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "odref" "" "" "${z_desc}"
276	}
277	
278	buv_ipv4_enroll() {
279	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
280	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
281	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "ipv4" "" "" "${z_desc}"
282	}
283	
284	buv_port_enroll() {
285	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
286	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
287	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "port" "" "" "${z_desc}"
288	}
289	
290	# Public enrollment functions — list types
291	
292	buv_list_string_enroll() {
293	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
294	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
295	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
296	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
297	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_string" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
298	}
299	
300	buv_list_ipv4_enroll() {
301	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
302	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
303	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_ipv4" "" "" "${z_desc}"
304	}
305	
306	buv_list_gname_enroll() {
307	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
308	  local z_p1="${2:-}"
309	  local z_p2="${3:-}"
310	  local z_desc="${4:-}"
311	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_gname" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
312	}
313	
314	buv_list_cidr_enroll() {
315	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
316	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
317	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_cidr" "" "" "${z_desc}"
318	}
319	
320	buv_list_domain_enroll() {
321	  local z_varname="${1:-}"
322	  local z_desc="${2:-}"
323	  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_domain" "" "" "${z_desc}"
324	}
325	
326	# Internal check capture — validates a single enrolled variable by roll index.
327	# Returns error detail on stdout (empty = pass).
328	# Echo "${BUV_check_gated}" when gate doesn't match (caller decides skip behavior).
329	# Echo "${BUV_check_fail}<detail>" on validation failure.
330	zbuv_check_capture() {
331	  zbuv_sentinel
332	
333	  local z_idx="${1:-}"
334	  local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"
335	  local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_idx]}"
336	  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
337	  local z_gate_val="${z_buv_gate_val_roll[$z_idx]}"
338	  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"
339	  local z_p2="${z_buv_p2_roll[$z_idx]}"
340	
341	  # Gating check — if gated and gate doesn't match, skip (pass)
342	  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
343	    local z_gate_actual="${!z_gate_var:-}"
344	    if test "${z_gate_actual}" != "${z_gate_val}"; then
345	      echo "${BUV_check_gated}"
346	      return 0
347	    fi
348	  fi
349	
350	  # Unset detection — distinguish "not set" from "set but empty"
351	  if test -z "${!z_varname+x}"; then
352	    case "${z_type}" in
353	      string|secret|gname)
354	        if test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then return 0; fi ;;
355	      list_string|list_ipv4|list_gname|list_cidr|list_domain)
356	        return 0 ;;
357	    esac
358	    echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} is not set (missing from .env?)"
359	    return 0
360	  fi
361	
362	  local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
363	
364	  case "${z_type}" in
365	
366	    string|secret)
367	      if test "${z_p1}" = "0" && test -z "${z_val}"; then
368	        return 0
369	      fi
370	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
371	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
372	        return 0
373	      fi
374	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
375	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
376	        return 0
377	      fi
378	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
379	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
380	        return 0
381	      fi
382	      ;;
383	
384	    xname)
385	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
386	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
387	        return 0
388	      fi
389	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
390	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
391	        return 0
392	      fi
393	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
394	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
395	        return 0
396	      fi
397	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$ ]] || {
398	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must start with letter and contain only letters, numbers, underscore, hyphen, got '${z_val}'"
399	        return 0
400	      }
401	      ;;
402	
403	    gname)
404	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
405	        if test "${z_p1}" -gt 0; then
406	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
407	          return 0
408	        fi
409	        return 0
410	      fi
411	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
412	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
413	        return 0
414	      fi
415	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
416	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
417	        return 0
418	      fi
419	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ ]] || {
420	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; start with a letter; end with letter/digit), got '${z_val}'"
421	        return 0
422	      }
423	      ;;
424	
425	    fqin)
426	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
427	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
428	        return 0
429	      fi
430	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
431	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
432	        return 0
433	      fi
434	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
435	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
436	        return 0
437	      fi
438	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9:._/@-]*$ ]] || {
439	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must start with letter/number and contain only letters, numbers, colons, dots, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, at-signs, got '${z_val}'"
440	        return 0
441	      }
442	      ;;
443	
444	    bool)
445	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
446	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
447	        return 0
448	      fi
449	      if test "${z_val}" != "0" && test "${z_val}" != "1"; then
450	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be 0 or 1, got: '${z_val}'"
451	        return 0
452	      fi
453	      ;;
454	
455	    enum)
456	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
457	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
458	        return 0
459	      fi
460	      local z_choice
461	      local z_found=0
462	      for z_choice in ${z_p1}; do
463	        if test "${z_val}" = "${z_choice}"; then
464	          z_found=1
465	          break
466	        fi
467	      done
468	      if test "${z_found}" = "0"; then
469	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be one of: ${z_p1}, got '${z_val}'"
470	        return 0
471	      fi
472	      ;;
473	
474	    decimal)
475	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
476	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
477	        return 0
478	      fi
479	      if test "${z_val}" -ge "${z_p1}" && test "${z_val}" -le "${z_p2}"; then
480	        return 0
481	      fi
482	      echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} value '${z_val}' must be between ${z_p1} and ${z_p2}"
483	      return 0
484	      ;;
485	
486	    odref)
487	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
488	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
489	        return 0
490	      fi
491	      local z_re='^[a-z0-9.-]+(:[0-9]{2,5})?/([a-z0-9._-]+/)*[a-z0-9._-]+@sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$'
492	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ${z_re} ]] || {
493	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} has invalid image reference format (require host[:port]/repo@sha256:<64hex>), got '${z_val}'"
494	        return 0
495	      }
496	      ;;
497	
498	    ipv4)
499	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
500	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
501	        return 0
502	      fi
503	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$ ]] || {
504	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} has invalid IPv4 format: '${z_val}'"
505	        return 0
506	      }
507	      ;;
508	
509	    port)
510	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
511	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
512	        return 0
513	      fi
514	      if test "${z_val}" -ge 1 && test "${z_val}" -le 65535; then
515	        return 0
516	      fi
517	      echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} value '${z_val}' must be between 1 and 65535"
518	      return 0
519	      ;;
520	
521	    list_string)
522	      local z_item
523	      local z_item_num=0
524	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
525	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
526	        if test "${#z_item}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
527	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
528	          return 0
529	        fi
530	        if test "${#z_item}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
531	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
532	          return 0
533	        fi
534	      done
535	      ;;
536	
537	    list_ipv4)
538	      local z_item
539	      local z_item_num=0
540	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
541	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
542	        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$ ]] || {
543	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid IPv4 format: '${z_item}'"
544	          return 0
545	        }
546	      done
547	      ;;
548	
549	    list_gname)
550	      local z_item
551	      local z_item_num=0
552	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
553	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
554	        if test "${#z_item}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
555	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
556	          return 0
557	        fi
558	        if test "${#z_item}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
559	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
560	          return 0
561	        fi
562	        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ ]] || {
563	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$, got '${z_item}'"
564	          return 0
565	        }
566	      done
567	      ;;
568	
569	    list_cidr)
570	      local z_item
571	      local z_item_num=0
572	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
573	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
574	        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}/[0-9]{1,2}$ ]] || {
575	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid CIDR format: '${z_item}'"
576	          return 0
577	        }
578	      done
579	      ;;
580	
581	    list_domain)
582	      local z_item
583	      local z_item_num=0
584	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
585	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
586	        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9.-]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$ ]] || {
587	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid domain format: '${z_item}'"
588	          return 0
589	        }
590	      done
591	      ;;
592	
593	    *)
594	      echo "${BUV_check_fail}unknown type: ${z_type}"
595	      return 0
596	      ;;
597	
598	  esac
599	}
600	
601	# buv_hallmark_format VALUE — validate hallmark string matches [cbg]YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS
602	# Returns 0 on valid, dies on invalid. Pass empty string to skip (optional fields).
603	buv_hallmark_format() {
604	  local z_val="${1:-}"
605	  test -n "${z_val}" || return 0
606	  [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[cbg][0-9]{12}-r[0-9]{12}$ ]] \
607	    || buc_die "Invalid hallmark format: '${z_val}' (expected [cbg]YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS)"
608	}
609	
610	# buv_scope_sentinel SCOPE PREFIX — die if any PREFIX_ vars exist that are not enrolled in SCOPE
611	# Usage: buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_
612	buv_scope_sentinel() {
613	  zbuv_sentinel
614	
615	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
616	  local z_prefix="${2:-}"
617	  test -n "${z_scope}"  || buc_die "buv_scope_sentinel: scope required"
618	  test -n "${z_prefix}" || buc_die "buv_scope_sentinel: prefix required"
619	
620	  # Build lookup string from enrolled varnames for this scope
621	  local z_known=" "
622	  local z_i
623	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
624	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
625	    z_known="${z_known}${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]} "
626	  done
627	
628	  # Scan environment for unexpected vars with this prefix
629	  local z_unexpected=()
630	  local z_var
631	  for z_var in $(compgen -v "${z_prefix}"); do
632	    case "${z_known}" in
633	      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
634	      *) z_unexpected+=("${z_var}") ;;
635	    esac
636	  done
637	
638	  if test "${#z_unexpected[@]}" -gt 0; then
639	    buc_die "Unexpected ${z_prefix}* variables not enrolled in ${z_scope}: ${z_unexpected[*]}"
640	  fi
641	}
642	
643	# buv_docker_env SCOPE ARRAY_VAR — populate ARRAY_VAR with -e VARNAME=val pairs for all enrolled vars in SCOPE
644	# Usage: buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
645	buv_docker_env() {
646	  zbuv_sentinel
647	
648	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
649	  local z_array_var="${2:-}"
650	  test -n "${z_scope}"     || buc_die "buv_docker_env: scope required"
651	  test -n "${z_array_var}" || buc_die "buv_docker_env: array variable name required"
652	  [[ "${z_array_var}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
653	    || buc_die "buv_docker_env: invalid array variable name: '${z_array_var}'"
654	
655	  eval "${z_array_var}=()"
656	
657	  local z_i
658	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
659	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
660	    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
661	    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
662	    eval "${z_array_var}+=(\"-e\" \"${z_varname}=${z_val}\")"
663	  done
664	}
665	
666	# buv_vet SCOPE — iterate all enrolled vars in scope; die on first failure
667	buv_vet() {
668	  zbuv_sentinel
669	
670	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
671	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_vet: scope required"
672	
673	  local z_i
674	  local z_err
675	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
676	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
677	    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
678	    test -z "${z_err}" || test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}" || buc_die "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
679	  done
680	}
681	
682	# buv_lock SCOPE — make all enrolled variables in scope readonly
683	# Call after enforce succeeds. Prevents downstream mutation of validated config.
684	buv_lock() {
685	  zbuv_sentinel
686	
687	  local -r z_scope="${1:-}"
688	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_lock: scope required"
689	
690	  local z_i
691	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
692	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
693	    readonly "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
694	  done
695	}
696	
697	buv_export_and_lock() {
698	  zbuv_sentinel
699	
700	  local -r z_scope="${1:-}"
701	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_export_and_lock: scope required"
702	
703	  local z_i
704	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
705	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
706	    export "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
707	    readonly "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
708	  done
709	}
710	
711	# buv_report SCOPE "Label" — rich per-variable display; returns non-zero if any failed
712	buv_report() {
713	  zbuv_sentinel
714	
715	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
716	  local z_label="${2:-}"
717	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_report: scope required"
718	  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_report: label required"
719	
720	  local z_any_failed=0
721	
722	  buc_step "${z_label}"
723	
724	  local z_i
725	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
726	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
727	
728	    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
729	    local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
730	    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
731	    local z_err
732	
733	    # Secret redaction — replace display value before PASS/FAIL output
734	    local z_display_val="${z_val}"
735	    if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_val}"; then
736	      z_display_val="(redacted — ${#z_val} chars)"
737	    fi
738	
739	    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
740	    if test -z "${z_err}"; then
741	      buc_step "  PASS  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]"
742	    elif test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}"; then
743	      buc_step "  SKIP  ${z_varname} (gated)"
744	    else
745	      buc_step "  FAIL  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
746	      z_any_failed=1
747	    fi
748	  done
749	
750	  return "${z_any_failed}"
751	}
752	
753	# zbuv_group_gate_recite SCOPE TITLE — look up group gate from registry
754	# Sets ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR and ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL (empty if ungated).
755	zbuv_group_gate_recite() {
756	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
757	  local z_title="${2:-}"
758	
759	  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR=""
760	  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL=""
761	
762	  local z_s
763	  for z_s in "${!z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]}"; do
764	    if test "${z_buv_grp_scope_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_scope}" \
765	      && test "${z_buv_grp_title_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_title}"; then
766	      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR="${z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[$z_s]}"
767	      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL="${z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[$z_s]}"
768	      return 0
769	    fi
770	  done
771	}
772	
773	# zbuv_req_status INDEX — derive req/opt/cond from enrollment data
774	# Sets ZBUV_REQ_STATUS.
775	zbuv_req_status() {
776	  local z_idx="${1:-}"
777	  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
778	  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"
779	
780	  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
781	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="cond"
782	  elif test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then
783	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="opt"
784	  else
785	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="req"
786	  fi
787	}
788	
789	# buv_render SCOPE "Label" — render all enrolled vars via bupr_ presentation
790	# Walks enrollment rolls grouped by group, applying group-level gates.
791	# Requires bupr (PresentationRegime) to be kindled.
792	buv_render() {
793	  zbuv_sentinel
794	  zbupr_sentinel
795	
796	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
797	  local z_label="${2:-}"
798	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_render: scope required"
799	  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_render: label required"
800	
801	  local z_current_group=""
802	  local z_i
803	  local z_group=""
804	  local z_varname=""
805	  local z_type=""
806	  local z_desc=""
807	
808	  echo ""
809	  echo "${BUC_white}${z_label}${BUC_reset}"
810	  echo ""
811	
812	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
813	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
814	
815	    z_group="${z_buv_group_roll[$z_i]}"
816	    z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
817	    z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
818	    z_desc="${z_buv_desc_roll[$z_i]}"
819	
820	    # Group transition — close previous, open new
821	    if test "${z_group}" != "${z_current_group}"; then
822	      if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
823	        bupr_section_end
824	      fi
825	      z_current_group="${z_group}"
826	
827	      zbuv_group_gate_recite "${z_scope}" "${z_group}"
828	      if test -n "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}"; then
829	        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL}"
830	      else
831	        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}"
832	      fi
833	    fi
834	
835	    zbuv_req_status "${z_i}"
836	    bupr_section_item "${z_varname}" "${z_type}" "${ZBUV_REQ_STATUS}" "${z_desc}"
837	  done
838	
839	  # Close final group
840	  if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
841	    bupr_section_end
842	  fi
843	}
844	
845	# eof
846	
847

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Validation Utility Library
# Compatible with Bash 3.2 (e.g., macOS default shell)

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUV_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUV_INCLUDED=1

# Literal constants — check capture output protocol markers
BUV_check_gated="gated"
BUV_check_fail="fail:"

# Source the console utility library
ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
source "${ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

buv_file_exists() {
  local z_filepath="${1:-}"
  test -f "${z_filepath}" || buc_die "Required file not found: ${z_filepath}"
}

buv_dir_exists() {
  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
}

buv_dir_empty() {
  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
  local z_check_file
  z_check_file=$(mktemp)
  find "${z_dirpath}" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -print -quit > "${z_check_file}"
  test ! -s "${z_check_file}" || { rm -f "${z_check_file}"; buc_die "Directory must be empty: ${z_dirpath}"; }
  rm -f "${z_check_file}"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enrollment infrastructure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

zbuv_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buv already kindled"

  # Enrollment rolls (9 parallel arrays)
  z_buv_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_varname_roll=()
  z_buv_type_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
  z_buv_p1_roll=()
  z_buv_p2_roll=()
  z_buv_group_roll=()
  z_buv_desc_roll=()

  # Group registry rolls (4 parallel arrays, foreign-keyed by group title)
  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()

  # Mutable kindle state: regime context (set by buv_regime_enroll / buv_group_enroll / buv_gate_enroll)
  z_buv_current_scope=""
  z_buv_current_group=""
  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
  z_buv_current_gate_val=""

  readonly ZBUV_KINDLED=1
}

zbuv_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buv not kindled - call zbuv_kindle first"
}

# Test support: reset enrollment state without re-kindling
zbuv_reset_enrollment() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  # Clear enrollment rolls
  z_buv_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_varname_roll=()
  z_buv_type_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
  z_buv_p1_roll=()
  z_buv_p2_roll=()
  z_buv_group_roll=()
  z_buv_desc_roll=()

  # Clear group registry rolls
  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()

  # Reset regime context state
  z_buv_current_scope=""
  z_buv_current_group=""
  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
}

# Regime context setters — called during kindle to establish enrollment scope

# buv_regime_enroll SCOPE — set current enrollment scope
# Validates that SCOPE is non-empty. All subsequent enroll calls use this scope
# until another buv_regime_enroll is called.
buv_regime_enroll() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_regime_enroll: scope required"
  z_buv_current_scope="${z_scope}"
  z_buv_current_group=""
  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
}

# buv_group_enroll TITLE — set current group context
# Creates a group registry entry. All subsequent enroll calls are tagged with
# this group title until the next buv_group_enroll call.
# Resets gate context — use buv_gate_enroll after this to gate items.
buv_group_enroll() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  test -n "${z_buv_current_scope}" || buc_die "buv_group_enroll: call buv_regime_enroll first"

  local z_title="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_title}" || buc_die "buv_group_enroll: title required"

  z_buv_grp_scope_roll+=("${z_buv_current_scope}")
  z_buv_grp_title_roll+=("${z_title}")
  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll+=("")
  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll+=("")

  z_buv_current_group="${z_title}"
  z_buv_current_group_idx=$(( ${#z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]} - 1 ))
  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
  z_buv_current_gate_val=""
}

# buv_gate_enroll GATE_VAR GATE_VAL — set gate context within current group
# All subsequent variable enrolls in this group are gated by this condition.
# Also registers the gate on the current group for render section headers.
buv_gate_enroll() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  test -n "${z_buv_current_group}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: call buv_group_enroll first"

  local z_gate_var="${1:-}"
  local z_gate_val="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_gate_var}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: gate variable required"
  test -n "${z_gate_val}" || buc_die "buv_gate_enroll: gate value required"

  z_buv_current_gate_var="${z_gate_var}"
  z_buv_current_gate_val="${z_gate_val}"

  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[z_buv_current_group_idx]="${z_gate_var}"
  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[z_buv_current_group_idx]="${z_gate_val}"
}

# Internal enrollment helper — all public enroll functions delegate here
# Usage: zbuv_enroll VARNAME TYPE P1 P2 DESC
# Gate context inherited from buv_gate_enroll; group from buv_group_enroll.
zbuv_enroll() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_type="${2:-}"
  local z_p1="${3:-}"
  local z_p2="${4:-}"
  local z_desc="${5:-}"

  test -n "${z_buv_current_scope}" || buc_die "zbuv_enroll: call buv_regime_enroll first"
  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] || buc_die "zbuv_enroll: invalid variable name: '${z_varname}'"

  z_buv_scope_roll+=("${z_buv_current_scope}")
  z_buv_varname_roll+=("${z_varname}")
  z_buv_type_roll+=("${z_type}")
  z_buv_gate_var_roll+=("${z_buv_current_gate_var}")
  z_buv_gate_val_roll+=("${z_buv_current_gate_val}")
  z_buv_p1_roll+=("${z_p1}")
  z_buv_p2_roll+=("${z_p2}")
  z_buv_group_roll+=("${z_buv_current_group}")
  z_buv_desc_roll+=("${z_desc}")
}

# Public enrollment functions — scalar types

# Internal check capture — validates a single enrolled variable by roll index.
# Returns error detail on stdout (empty = pass).
# Echo "${BUV_check_gated}" when gate doesn't match (caller decides skip behavior).
# Echo "${BUV_check_fail}<detail>" on validation failure.
zbuv_check_capture() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_idx="${1:-}"
  local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"
  local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_idx]}"
  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
  local z_gate_val="${z_buv_gate_val_roll[$z_idx]}"
  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"
  local z_p2="${z_buv_p2_roll[$z_idx]}"

  # Gating check — if gated and gate doesn't match, skip (pass)
  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
    local z_gate_actual="${!z_gate_var:-}"
    if test "${z_gate_actual}" != "${z_gate_val}"; then
      echo "${BUV_check_gated}"
      return 0
    fi
  fi

  # Unset detection — distinguish "not set" from "set but empty"
  if test -z "${!z_varname+x}"; then
    case "${z_type}" in
      string|secret|gname)
        if test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then return 0; fi ;;
      list_string|list_ipv4|list_gname|list_cidr|list_domain)
        return 0 ;;
    esac
    echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} is not set (missing from .env?)"
    return 0
  fi

  local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"

  case "${z_type}" in

    string|secret)
      if test "${z_p1}" = "0" && test -z "${z_val}"; then
        return 0
      fi
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      ;;

    xname)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$ ]] || {
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must start with letter and contain only letters, numbers, underscore, hyphen, got '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      }
      ;;

    gname)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        if test "${z_p1}" -gt 0; then
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
          return 0
        fi
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ ]] || {
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; start with a letter; end with letter/digit), got '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      }
      ;;

    fqin)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
        return 0
      fi
      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9:._/@-]*$ ]] || {
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must start with letter/number and contain only letters, numbers, colons, dots, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, at-signs, got '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      }
      ;;

    bool)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${z_val}" != "0" && test "${z_val}" != "1"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be 0 or 1, got: '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      fi
      ;;

    enum)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      local z_choice
      local z_found=0
      for z_choice in ${z_p1}; do
        if test "${z_val}" = "${z_choice}"; then
          z_found=1
          break
        fi
      done
      if test "${z_found}" = "0"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be one of: ${z_p1}, got '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      fi
      ;;

    decimal)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${z_val}" -ge "${z_p1}" && test "${z_val}" -le "${z_p2}"; then
        return 0
      fi
      echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} value '${z_val}' must be between ${z_p1} and ${z_p2}"
      return 0
      ;;

    odref)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      local z_re='^[a-z0-9.-]+(:[0-9]{2,5})?/([a-z0-9._-]+/)*[a-z0-9._-]+@sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$'
      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ${z_re} ]] || {
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} has invalid image reference format (require host[:port]/repo@sha256:<64hex>), got '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      }
      ;;

    ipv4)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$ ]] || {
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} has invalid IPv4 format: '${z_val}'"
        return 0
      }
      ;;

    port)
      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
        return 0
      fi
      if test "${z_val}" -ge 1 && test "${z_val}" -le 65535; then
        return 0
      fi
      echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} value '${z_val}' must be between 1 and 65535"
      return 0
      ;;

    list_string)
      local z_item
      local z_item_num=0
      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
        if test "${#z_item}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
          return 0
        fi
        if test "${#z_item}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
          return 0
        fi
      done
      ;;

    list_ipv4)
      local z_item
      local z_item_num=0
      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$ ]] || {
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid IPv4 format: '${z_item}'"
          return 0
        }
      done
      ;;

    list_gname)
      local z_item
      local z_item_num=0
      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
        if test "${#z_item}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
          return 0
        fi
        if test "${#z_item}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
          return 0
        fi
        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ ]] || {
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$, got '${z_item}'"
          return 0
        }
      done
      ;;

    list_cidr)
      local z_item
      local z_item_num=0
      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}/[0-9]{1,2}$ ]] || {
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid CIDR format: '${z_item}'"
          return 0
        }
      done
      ;;

    list_domain)
      local z_item
      local z_item_num=0
      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9.-]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$ ]] || {
          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid domain format: '${z_item}'"
          return 0
        }
      done
      ;;

    *)
      echo "${BUV_check_fail}unknown type: ${z_type}"
      return 0
      ;;

  esac
}

# buv_hallmark_format VALUE — validate hallmark string matches [cbg]YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS
# Returns 0 on valid, dies on invalid. Pass empty string to skip (optional fields).
buv_hallmark_format() {
  local z_val="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_val}" || return 0
  [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[cbg][0-9]{12}-r[0-9]{12}$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid hallmark format: '${z_val}' (expected [cbg]YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS)"
}

# buv_scope_sentinel SCOPE PREFIX — die if any PREFIX_ vars exist that are not enrolled in SCOPE
# Usage: buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_
buv_scope_sentinel() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_prefix="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}"  || buc_die "buv_scope_sentinel: scope required"
  test -n "${z_prefix}" || buc_die "buv_scope_sentinel: prefix required"

  # Build lookup string from enrolled varnames for this scope
  local z_known=" "
  local z_i
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
    z_known="${z_known}${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]} "
  done

  # Scan environment for unexpected vars with this prefix
  local z_unexpected=()
  local z_var
  for z_var in $(compgen -v "${z_prefix}"); do
    case "${z_known}" in
      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
      *) z_unexpected+=("${z_var}") ;;
    esac
  done

  if test "${#z_unexpected[@]}" -gt 0; then
    buc_die "Unexpected ${z_prefix}* variables not enrolled in ${z_scope}: ${z_unexpected[*]}"
  fi
}

# buv_docker_env SCOPE ARRAY_VAR — populate ARRAY_VAR with -e VARNAME=val pairs for all enrolled vars in SCOPE
# Usage: buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
buv_docker_env() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_array_var="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}"     || buc_die "buv_docker_env: scope required"
  test -n "${z_array_var}" || buc_die "buv_docker_env: array variable name required"
  [[ "${z_array_var}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
    || buc_die "buv_docker_env: invalid array variable name: '${z_array_var}'"

  eval "${z_array_var}=()"

  local z_i
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
    eval "${z_array_var}+=(\"-e\" \"${z_varname}=${z_val}\")"
  done
}

# buv_vet SCOPE — iterate all enrolled vars in scope; die on first failure
buv_vet() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_vet: scope required"

  local z_i
  local z_err
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
    test -z "${z_err}" || test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}" || buc_die "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
  done
}

# buv_lock SCOPE — make all enrolled variables in scope readonly
# Call after enforce succeeds. Prevents downstream mutation of validated config.
buv_lock() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local -r z_scope="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_lock: scope required"

  local z_i
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
    readonly "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
  done
}

buv_export_and_lock() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local -r z_scope="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_export_and_lock: scope required"

  local z_i
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
    export "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
    readonly "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
  done
}

# buv_report SCOPE "Label" — rich per-variable display; returns non-zero if any failed
buv_report() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_label="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_report: scope required"
  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_report: label required"

  local z_any_failed=0

  buc_step "${z_label}"

  local z_i
  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue

    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
    local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
    local z_err

    # Secret redaction — replace display value before PASS/FAIL output
    local z_display_val="${z_val}"
    if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_val}"; then
      z_display_val="(redacted — ${#z_val} chars)"
    fi

    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
    if test -z "${z_err}"; then
      buc_step "  PASS  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]"
    elif test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}"; then
      buc_step "  SKIP  ${z_varname} (gated)"
    else
      buc_step "  FAIL  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
      z_any_failed=1
    fi
  done

  return "${z_any_failed}"
}

# zbuv_group_gate_recite SCOPE TITLE — look up group gate from registry
# Sets ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR and ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL (empty if ungated).
zbuv_group_gate_recite() {
  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_title="${2:-}"

  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR=""
  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL=""

  local z_s
  for z_s in "${!z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    if test "${z_buv_grp_scope_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_scope}" \
      && test "${z_buv_grp_title_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_title}"; then
      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR="${z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[$z_s]}"
      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL="${z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[$z_s]}"
      return 0
    fi
  done
}

# zbuv_req_status INDEX — derive req/opt/cond from enrollment data
# Sets ZBUV_REQ_STATUS.
zbuv_req_status() {
  local z_idx="${1:-}"
  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"

  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="cond"
  elif test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then
    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="opt"
  else
    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="req"
  fi
}

# buv_render SCOPE "Label" — render all enrolled vars via bupr_ presentation
# Walks enrollment rolls grouped by group, applying group-level gates.
# Requires bupr (PresentationRegime) to be kindled.
buv_render() {
  zbuv_sentinel
  zbupr_sentinel

  local z_scope="${1:-}"
  local z_label="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_render: scope required"
  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_render: label required"

  local z_current_group=""
  local z_i
  local z_group=""
  local z_varname=""
  local z_type=""
  local z_desc=""

  echo ""
  echo "${BUC_white}${z_label}${BUC_reset}"
  echo ""

  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue

    z_group="${z_buv_group_roll[$z_i]}"
    z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
    z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
    z_desc="${z_buv_desc_roll[$z_i]}"

    # Group transition — close previous, open new
    if test "${z_group}" != "${z_current_group}"; then
      if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
        bupr_section_end
      fi
      z_current_group="${z_group}"

      zbuv_group_gate_recite "${z_scope}" "${z_group}"
      if test -n "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}"; then
        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL}"
      else
        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}"
      fi
    fi

    zbuv_req_status "${z_i}"
    bupr_section_item "${z_varname}" "${z_type}" "${ZBUV_REQ_STATUS}" "${z_desc}"
  done

  # Close final group
  if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
    bupr_section_end
  fi
}

# eof

950	+
951	{burn_regime}
952	files live under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/burn.env`.
953	+
954	{burn_regime}
955	is the sole home of node address-of-record per
956	{burn_host_singularity};
957	{burp_regime}
958	instances reference it via
959	{burp_node}.
960	
961	[[burn_host]]
962	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
963	{burn_host}::
964	IP address or hostname of the remote node.
965	
966	[[burn_platform]]
967	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
968	{burn_platform}::
969	OS family of the node.
970	Used by handbook diagnostics; the
971	{bus_jurisdiction}
972	implementation in
973	{bujb_jurisdiction}
974	selects shell mappings by garrison shell-letter rather than by node platform.
975	Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.
976	
977	=== BURN/BURP Identifier Vocabulary
978	
979	The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
980	BURN/BURP model.
981	
982	[[burn_viceroyalty]]
983	//axl_voices axo_entity
984	{burn_viceroyalty}::
985	The minted identifier of a
986	{burn_regime}
987	instance.
988	{burp_regime}
989	instances reference a node by its
990	{burn_viceroyalty}.
991	+
992	The
993	{burn_viceroyalty}
994	is conveyed by the directory containing the
995	{burn_regime}
996	assignment file;
997	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
998	+
999	Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.
1000	
1001	[[burp_investiture]]
1002	//axl_voices axo_entity
1003	{burp_investiture}::
1004	The minted identifier of a
1005	{burp_regime}
1006	instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
1007	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1008	+
1009	The
1010	{burp_investiture}
1011	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1012	{burp_regime}
1013	assignment file;
1014	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1015	+
1016	Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.
1017	
1018	[[burn_host_singularity]]
1019	//axl_voices axk_premise
1020	{burn_host_singularity}::
1021	The host of a node lives only in
1022	{burn_regime}.
1023	{burp_regime}
1024	instances reach the host by reference (via
1025	{burp_node})
1026	and never duplicate it.
1027	A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
1028	credentials never re-state it.
1029	
1030	[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
1031	//axl_voices axk_premise
1032	{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
1033	The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
1034	The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
1035	distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
1036	keys on the operator's station.
1037	Regime files carry only the public-key line;
1038	private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
1039	BUK procedures.
1040	
1041	=== BURP Regime
1042	
1043	[[burp_prefix]]
1044	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1045	{burp_prefix}::
1046	Bash Utility Regime Privileged
1047	
1048	[[burp_regime]]
1049	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
1050	{burp_regime}::
1051	Per-station-user credential regime carrying both privileged (admin)
1052	and workload key references for a single
1053	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1054	A
1055	{burp_regime}
1056	instance is identified by its
1057	{burp_investiture}
1058	(directory name).
1059	+
1060	{burp_regime}
1061	files live under
1062	`.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/burp.env`.
1063	+
1064	{burp_regime}
1065	references its node by
1066	{burp_node}
1067	per
1068	{burn_host_singularity}.
1069	+
1070	Public keys are derived from private keys at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
1071	neither the privileged nor the workload pubkey is stored in
1072	{burp_regime}.
1073	Encrypted (passphrase-protected) private keys are not supported because
1074	`ssh-keygen -y` would prompt interactively and hang the ceremony.
1075	
1076	[[burp_node]]
1077	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1078	{burp_node}::
1079	Reference to a
1080	{burn_viceroyalty}
1081	— the node this investiture is granted over.
1082	Resolves to the
1083	{burn_regime}
1084	file at the directory bearing this name.
1085	
1086	[[burp_privileged_user]]
1087	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1088	{burp_privileged_user}::
1089	Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as for the
1090	privileged garrison ceremony
1091	(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
1092	Linux/Mac).
1093	Garrison runs against this account to provision the workload account.
1094	+
1095	Some platforms permit privileged usernames containing spaces
1096	(e.g., `b hyslop`); the implementation must quote correctly at every
1097	expansion.
1098	
1099	[[burp_privileged_key_file]]
1100	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
1101	{burp_privileged_key_file}::
1102	Path to the operator-managed SSH private key authorized as
1103	{burp_privileged_user}
1104	on the node per
1105	{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
1106	The matching public key is derived at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
1107	no separate pubkey path is stored.
1108	+
1109	{busn_garrison}
1110	authenticates using this key only.
1111	First-time admin trust is established by
1112	{busn_fenestrate}
1113	on Windows nodes, or operator-manually
1114	(e.g., `ssh-copy-id`) on Linux/Mac nodes.
1115	{busn_fenestrate}
1116	itself uses
1117	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`
1118	on its phase-1 admin SSH session so that on a fresh Windows node ssh
1119	prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password once; fenestrate then
1120	installs the admin pubkey and hardens sshd, after which all
1121	subsequent admin authentication is by key alone.

1125	{burp_workload_key_file}::

1127	to authenticate inbound (from the operator's station) and outbound
1128	(to GitHub or other services).
1129	Garrison copies this private key onto the remote workload account at a
1130	shell-letter-specific path hardcoded in

1136	(e.g., GitHub deploy keys) out-of-band; garrison cannot validate that

file.content:
+
{burn_regime}
files live under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/burn.env`.
+
{burn_regime}
is the sole home of node address-of-record per
{burn_host_singularity};
{burp_regime}
instances reference it via
{burp_node}.

[[burn_host]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_host}::
IP address or hostname of the remote node.

[[burn_platform]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_platform}::
OS family of the node.
Used by handbook diagnostics; the
{bus_jurisdiction}
implementation in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
selects shell mappings by garrison shell-letter rather than by node platform.
Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.

=== BURN/BURP Identifier Vocabulary

The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
BURN/BURP model.

[[burn_viceroyalty]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burn_viceroyalty}::
The minted identifier of a
{burn_regime}
instance.
{burp_regime}
instances reference a node by its
{burn_viceroyalty}.
+
The
{burn_viceroyalty}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burn_regime}
assignment file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.

[[burp_investiture]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burp_investiture}::
The minted identifier of a
{burp_regime}
instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
{burn_viceroyalty}.
+
The
{burp_investiture}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burp_regime}
assignment file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.

[[burn_host_singularity]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{burn_host_singularity}::
The host of a node lives only in
{burn_regime}.
{burp_regime}
instances reach the host by reference (via
{burp_node})
and never duplicate it.
A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
credentials never re-state it.

[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
keys on the operator's station.
Regime files carry only the public-key line;
private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
BUK procedures.

=== BURP Regime

[[burp_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burp_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Privileged

[[burp_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burp_regime}::
Per-station-user credential regime carrying both privileged (admin)
and workload key references for a single
{burn_viceroyalty}.
A
{burp_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burp_investiture}
(directory name).
+
{burp_regime}
files live under
`.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/burp.env`.
+
{burp_regime}
references its node by
{burp_node}
per
{burn_host_singularity}.
+
Public keys are derived from private keys at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
neither the privileged nor the workload pubkey is stored in
{burp_regime}.
Encrypted (passphrase-protected) private keys are not supported because
`ssh-keygen -y` would prompt interactively and hang the ceremony.

[[burp_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burp_node}::
Reference to a
{burn_viceroyalty}
— the node this investiture is granted over.
Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name.

[[burp_privileged_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burp_privileged_user}::
Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as for the
privileged garrison ceremony
(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
Linux/Mac).
Garrison runs against this account to provision the workload account.
+
Some platforms permit privileged usernames containing spaces
(e.g., `b hyslop`); the implementation must quote correctly at every
expansion.

[[burp_privileged_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_privileged_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key authorized as
{burp_privileged_user}
on the node per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
The matching public key is derived at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
no separate pubkey path is stored.
+
{busn_garrison}
authenticates using this key only.
First-time admin trust is established by
{busn_fenestrate}
on Windows nodes, or operator-manually
(e.g., `ssh-copy-id`) on Linux/Mac nodes.
{busn_fenestrate}
itself uses
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`
on its phase-1 admin SSH session so that on a fresh Windows node ssh
prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password once; fenestrate then
installs the admin pubkey and hardens sshd, after which all
subsequent admin authentication is by key alone.

[[burp_workload_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_workload_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key the workload account uses
to authenticate inbound (from the operator's station) and outbound
(to GitHub or other services).
Garrison copies this private key onto the remote workload account at a
shell-letter-specific path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction};
the matching public key is derived via `ssh-keygen -y` for
authorized_keys placement on both sides of the trip.
+
The workload pubkey must be pre-registered with any outbound service
(e.g., GitHub deploy keys) out-of-band; garrison cannot validate that
registration.

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.

content.subject: L1: Regime & Constants
content.description: Four-field BURP (BURP_VICEROYALTY, BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER, BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE, BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE), drop BURN_WORKLOADS, add BURC_WORKLOAD_USER (xname 1-32), mint bub/bube/bubep_* tree, place BUBC_platforms_* tinder constants, re-enroll BURN_PLATFORM with bubep_* values.

content.subject: L2: Module & Helper
content.description: Create bujb_jurisdiction.sh + bujb_cli.sh BCG-compliant. bujb_resolve_investiture as sole entrypoint exposing BUJB_RESOLVED_* globals. Resolve-time enforcement: BURP_VICEROYALTY registered in BURN, key files mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load, WSL distro = rbtww-main.

content.subject: L3: Tabtargets & Ceremonies
content.description: 14 tabtargets total (11 net-new + 3 reshape buw-rn[lrv]). Net-new: buw-rp[lrv], buw-jpF, buw-jpG[bcw], buw-jwk, buw-jwc, buw-jws, buw-hj0. Per-verb platform invariant assertions inline. Fenestrate two-phase ceremony per BUSJPF; garrison 6-step ceremony per BUSJG[BCW].

content.subject: L4: Spec & Integration
content.description: BUS0/BUSJG[BCW] spec the three command= directive strings; BUS0 drift sweep (:burp_node: → :burp_viceroyalty: + siblings). Retire colophons replaced by 14-target catalog. Rewire RBK Windows orchestrator (rbhw_windows.sh, rbw-HWd[cdw], rbw-h0, rbw-hw) to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.

content.subject: L5: Verification
content.description: bash -n green on all edited shell files; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh unchanged from baseline.

1	# BUK Privileged Investitures (BURP)
2	
3	Per-station-user privileged-credential profiles, keyed by `BURS_USER` from the station regime.
4	
5	## Structure
6	
7	```
8	.buk/rbmu_users/<BURS_USER>/<investiture>/burp.env
9	```
10	
11	Each subdirectory under a user is a BURP investiture — one privileged-credential identity granted over a node. The `<investiture>` is the directory name; it is not redundantly inscribed inside the file.
12	
13	## Fields
14	
15	```
16	BURP_NODE=<viceroyalty>          # references .buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>
17	BURP_USER=<remote-os-user>       # admin user authenticated by this investiture
18	BURP_SSH_PUBKEY='ssh-... ...'    # operator-managed admin keypair (public side)
19	BURP_KEY_FILE=<filename>         # SSH private key filename (empty = use investiture name)
20	BURP_COMMAND='<shell-command>'   # optional command= routing in administrators_authorized_keys
21	```
22	
23	## Git Safety
24	
25	BURP profiles contain **public** key material only (`BURP_SSH_PUBKEY`). Private keys are never stored here; they live at operator-managed paths.
26	
27	## Validation
28	
29	```bash
30	tt/buw-rpl.ListPrivilegeRegime.sh                # all investitures for current BURS_USER
31	tt/buw-rpv.ValidatePrivilegeRegime.sh <invest>   # single investiture
32	tt/buw-rpr.RenderPrivilegeRegime.sh <invest>     # show resolved fields
33	```
34	
35	## Companion: BURN Node Profiles
36	
37	Node-shape profiles (host, platform) live at `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env` — project-global, git-tracked. Each BURP investiture references a viceroyalty by `BURP_NODE`.
38

# BUK Privileged Investitures (BURP)

Per-station-user privileged-credential profiles, keyed by `BURS_USER` from the station regime.

## Structure

```
.buk/rbmu_users/<BURS_USER>/<investiture>/burp.env
```

Each subdirectory under a user is a BURP investiture — one privileged-credential identity granted over a node. The `<investiture>` is the directory name; it is not redundantly inscribed inside the file.

## Fields

```
BURP_NODE=<viceroyalty>          # references .buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>
BURP_USER=<remote-os-user>       # admin user authenticated by this investiture
BURP_SSH_PUBKEY='ssh-... ...'    # operator-managed admin keypair (public side)
BURP_KEY_FILE=<filename>         # SSH private key filename (empty = use investiture name)
BURP_COMMAND='<shell-command>'   # optional command= routing in administrators_authorized_keys
```

## Git Safety

BURP profiles contain **public** key material only (`BURP_SSH_PUBKEY`). Private keys are never stored here; they live at operator-managed paths.

## Validation

```bash
tt/buw-rpl.ListPrivilegeRegime.sh                # all investitures for current BURS_USER
tt/buw-rpv.ValidatePrivilegeRegime.sh <invest>   # single investiture
tt/buw-rpr.RenderPrivilegeRegime.sh <invest>     # show resolved fields
```

## Companion: BURN Node Profiles

Node-shape profiles (host, platform) live at `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env` — project-global, git-tracked. Each BURP investiture references a viceroyalty by `BURP_NODE`.

20	## Git Safety

33

# BUK Node Viceroyalties (BURN)

Per-node configuration profiles describing the shape of remote targets. Project-global, git-tracked, shared across station users.

## Structure

```
.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env
```

Each subdirectory is a BURN viceroyalty — one node identity. The `<viceroyalty>` is the directory name; it is not redundantly inscribed inside the file.

## Fields

```
BURN_HOST=<ip-or-hostname>       # IP address or hostname of the remote node
BURN_PLATFORM=<platform>         # linux | mac | cygwin | wsl | powershell | localhost
```

## Git Safety

BURN profiles carry no key material — they describe node shape only. Safe to commit; shared across station users.

## Validation

```bash
tt/buw-rnl.ListNodeRegime.sh                  # all viceroyalties
tt/buw-rnv.ValidateNodeRegime.sh <viceroyalty>  # single viceroyalty
tt/buw-rnr.RenderNodeRegime.sh <viceroyalty>    # show resolved fields
```

## Companion: BURP Privileged Investitures

Privileged credentials live at `.buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/burp.env` — per-station-user, operator-authored. Each BURP investiture references a viceroyalty by `BURP_NODE`.

At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.

{burc_workload_user}::
Project-wide convention name for the unprivileged workload OS user that
{busn_garrison}
provisions on every node — single source of truth, one workload user
per node.
+
Validation enforces a name-collision blacklist (against well-known
admin/service accounts) and platform-imposed length limits.

=== BURS Regime

{burs_regime}::
Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.

Local developer username.
Routes to the correct `.buk/rbmu_users/` subdirectory for
{burp_regime}
profiles.

[[burs_log_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burs_log_dir}::
Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.

=== BURN Regime

[[burn_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burn_regime}::
Per-node configuration regime describing the shape of a remote target.
A
{burn_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burn_viceroyalty}
(directory name) and is shared across station users (git-transported);
it carries no key material and changes rarely.
+

files live under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/burn.env`.
+
{burn_regime}
is the sole home of node address-of-record per

Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.

1530	under the current station user's tree.
1531	
1532	{busn_garrison}
1533	authenticates as
1534	{burp_privileged_user}
1535	using
1536	{burp_privileged_key_file}
1537	by key alone; admin trust is established beforehand by
1538	{busn_fenestrate}
1539	on Windows (which uses a one-time password fallback on its own
1540	phase-1 admin SSH session) or operator-manually on Linux/Mac.
1541	Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
1542	{burc_workload_user}
1543	(project-wide convention, single source of truth).
1544	
1545	=== Garrison-Destructive Model
1546	
1547	Every
1548	{busn_garrison}
1549	invocation wipes prior workload state on the node regardless of which
1550	{burp_investiture}
1551	or station user runs it.
1552	There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
1553	belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
1554	Garrison subsumes the workload-account lifecycle: there is no separate
1555	verb to materialize, remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every
1556	garrison rebuilds from a clean slate.
1557	+
1558	Admin-side sshd configuration is out of garrison's scope.
1559	{busn_fenestrate}
1560	owns Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening; on Linux/Mac the
1561	operator manages sshd directly.
1562	
1563	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1564	
1565	Fourteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, fenestrate, garrison
1566	ceremony, and workload dispatch.
1567	PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.
1568	
1569	==== Regime Configuration
1570	
1571	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1572	//axvo_tabtarget
1573	{bust_node_regime_list}::
1574	`buw-rnl` —
1575	List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.
1576	
1577	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1578	//axvo_tabtarget
1579	{bust_node_regime_render}::
1580	`buw-rnr` —
1581	Render a single {burn_regime} profile
1582	(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1583	
1584	[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
1585	//axvo_tabtarget
1586	{bust_node_regime_validate}::
1587	`buw-rnv` —
1588	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
1589	(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1590	
1591	[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
1592	//axvo_tabtarget
1593	{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
1594	`buw-rpl` —
1595	List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.
1596	
1597	[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
1598	//axvo_tabtarget
1599	{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
1600	`buw-rpr` —
1601	Render a single {burp_regime} profile
1602	(investiture passed as positional argument).
1603	
1604	[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
1605	//axvo_tabtarget
1606	{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
1607	`buw-rpv` —
1608	Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
1609	(investiture passed as positional argument).
1610	
1611	==== Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only)
1612	
1613	[[bust_fenestrate]]
1614	//axvo_tabtarget
1615	{bust_fenestrate}::
1616	`buw-jpF` —
1617	{busn_fenestrate} ceremony: hardens admin SSH trust and `sshd_config`
1618	on a Windows OpenSSH node (uniform; no shell-letter variant).
1619	Capital `F` marks this as a persistent-config-application tabtarget.
1620	
1621	==== Garrison (per shell-letter)

1623	[[bust_garrison_bash]]

1627	{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
1628	(Linux and macOS nodes).
1629	Capital `G` marks this as a persistent-config-application tabtarget.

under the current station user's tree.

{busn_garrison}
authenticates as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
by key alone; admin trust is established beforehand by
{busn_fenestrate}
on Windows (which uses a one-time password fallback on its own
phase-1 admin SSH session) or operator-manually on Linux/Mac.
Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
{burc_workload_user}
(project-wide convention, single source of truth).

=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every
{busn_garrison}
invocation wipes prior workload state on the node regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Garrison subsumes the workload-account lifecycle: there is no separate
verb to materialize, remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every
garrison rebuilds from a clean slate.
+
Admin-side sshd configuration is out of garrison's scope.
{busn_fenestrate}
owns Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening; on Linux/Mac the
operator manages sshd directly.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Fourteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, fenestrate, garrison
ceremony, and workload dispatch.
PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.

==== Regime Configuration

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget

List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only)

[[bust_fenestrate]]
//axvo_tabtarget

`buw-jpF` —
{busn_fenestrate} ceremony: hardens admin SSH trust and `sshd_config`
on a Windows OpenSSH node (uniform; no shell-letter variant).
Capital `F` marks this as a persistent-config-application tabtarget.

==== Garrison (per shell-letter)

[[bust_garrison_bash]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_bash}::
`buw-jpGb` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
(Linux and macOS nodes).
Capital `G` marks this as a persistent-config-application tabtarget.

content.content:
1480	hardens admin SSH trust and `sshd_config` on Windows OpenSSH nodes
1481	(operator-manual on Linux/Mac, e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); {busn_garrison}
1482	provisions and refreshes a workload account from an existing admin
1483	SSH foothold. The workload-side tabtargets
1484	({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
1485	dispatch work to that account.
1486	+
1487	{bus_jurisdiction}
1488	is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
1489	Its implementation seat is
1490	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
1491	
1492	[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
1493	{bujb_jurisdiction}::
1494	The BCG-compliant module
1495	(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
1496	that houses both
1497	{busn_fenestrate}
1498	and
1499	{busn_garrison}
1500	implementations and hardcodes the
1501	{bus_jurisdiction}
1502	contracts not expressed in regime data:

1504	(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;

1506	`w` = WSL)
1507	used by garrison on the workload account,

1509	the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`),
1510	and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive set used by
1511	fenestrate.
1512	+

1514	{burp_regime}
1515	carries no shell selection.
1516	
1517	=== Privileges
1518

1522	across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and

1524	A
1525	{burp_regime}

1527	the

file.content:
hardens admin SSH trust and `sshd_config` on Windows OpenSSH nodes
(operator-manual on Linux/Mac, e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); {busn_garrison}
provisions and refreshes a workload account from an existing admin
SSH foothold. The workload-side tabtargets
({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
dispatch work to that account.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
{bujb_jurisdiction}::
The BCG-compliant module
(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
that houses both
{busn_fenestrate}
and
{busn_garrison}
implementations and hardcodes the
{bus_jurisdiction}
contracts not expressed in regime data:
the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
`c` = Cygwin;
`w` = WSL)
used by garrison on the workload account,
the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`),
and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive set used by
fenestrate.
+
SSH transport mechanics live in code;
{burp_regime}
carries no shell selection.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
